objective: Desktop Java/Unidbg signer with configurable observed Android/native environment, unchanged Signer API, fixed onResume-to-sign order, strict device-reference equivalence, and an independently runnable native reimplementation where recoverable.
currentFocus: Expand native-reimplementation from the verified AES-256 and codeword primitives toward the complete 176-byte signer pipeline while preserving the now-passing Java/Unidbg strict reference gate.

evidence.strictDesktopReference: test-device-reference.sh exits 0 without ADB, Frida, an emulator, or a phone; SignerOneClick recursively matches the complete frozen Pixel 8 result and the raw signature is 176 bytes.
evidence.rootCause: libsigner.so+0x13063c scans signer function entries for an ARM64 LDR-literal-to-BR trampoline. The frozen true-device run had Frida Interceptor hooks on nOnResume/nSign, which causes the protected dispatcher to execute correction code 0x25.
evidence.configSurface: device.runtime.signerCodeTrampolineDetected is parsed by SignerOneClick, stored in DeviceProfile, propagated through AdjustSignatureRunner.Config, and modeled in Unidbg without running Frida.
evidence.codewordClosure: The strict path executes corrections 0x2b->0xd49d, 0x36->0xb5d3, 0x25->0xcacc, and 0x05->0x6ee6; context+0x54 becomes 0x6ee6cacc before downstream permutation.
evidence.nativeSourceSlice: native-reimplementation/build-and-test.sh independently compiles C++ with the host compiler and passes the Pixel first-block AES-256 vector plus the complete 16-halfword encoder recovered from libsigner.so+0x13531c.
evidence.regression: 33 Maven tests pass with zero failures/errors/skips. API contract, one-click determinism, external structured API, process isolation, Python compatibility contract, native C++ primitive test, and Pixel 8 strict reference all pass.
evidence.runtimeStability: generate-signer.sh prefers an already-installed macOS JDK 17 for signer execution, supports SIGNER_JAVA_BIN override, and falls back to java; no runtime is installed automatically.
evidence.remainingBoundary: Normal Java signing still executes the original ARM64 libsigner.so under Unidbg. The independently compiled C++ source does not yet produce the complete 176-byte result and is not a full SO replacement.
